RICHLAND BEAN BLOSSOM HEALTH CARE CENTER

CCN: 155523 · ELLETTSVILLE, IN 47429 · Monroe County

Address
5911 STATE ROAD 46, ELLETTSVILLE, IN 47429
Phone
8128766400
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
61 (82% of beds filled)

Non-profit corporation
Provider type

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.39 3.95 3.32 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.28 0.69 0.28 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.75 0.88 0.73 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.36 2.38 2.31 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.03 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.09 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.11
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.40
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.04
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
65.6%
annual
RN turnover
77.8%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.40
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.01
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-09 14 12 2 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-12 9 4 5 36 1 36
Total weighted health score
60.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (29)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
28 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-12-09 2025-12-16
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0574 The resident has the right to receive notices in a format and a language he or she understands. C 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-05-09 2025-06-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-09-24 2024-09-11
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-07-12 2024-08-07
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2024-07-12 2024-08-07
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-07-12 2024-08-07
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-07-12 2024-08-07
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-02-08 2024-02-23
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2024-02-08 2024-01-10
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0776 Provide timely, approved x-ray services, or have an agreement with an approved provider to obtain them. D 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-05-22 2023-06-23
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-03
